name: Melbourne (Victoria, Australia)

colors.symbolism: the Cross of St George and its cotises
the royal crown and the golden fleece

description: A white field quartered by the red Cross of St George (flanked by narrow red cotises), with a royal crown at the centre; the quarters bear a golden fleece on a red ring, a black bull, a three-masted ship, and a spouting whale — the arms of the City of Melbourne.
history: The flag is a banner of the City of Melbourne's coat of arms, granted by letters patent on 30 January 1940 and derived from the city's 1848 common seal. In 1970 the positions of the bull and the whale were exchanged so that the two land emblems occupy the upper row. Melbourne is the capital of Victoria.

features.role: Cross of Saint George (with red cotises) quartering the field
golden fleece on a red ring (wool industry)
